name: pygv
documentation:
# pygv

a minimal, scriptable genome browser for python built with [anywidget](https://github.com/manzt/anywidget)

## installation

**Here be dragons 🐉**

```sh
pip install pygv
```

## usage

```py
import pygv

# set the reference genome
pygv.ref("mm10")

# set the locus
pygv.locus("chr17:31,531,100-31,531,259")

# create a browser instance
pygv.browse("fragments.bed", "10x_cov.bw")
```

![igv.js in Jupyter Notebook](https://github.com/manzt/anywidget/assets/24403730/8aa77384-6d7c-422f-9238-37e06a0272f6)

That's it. By default, `pygv` infers the track and data-types by file
extension. If a file format has an index file, it must be specified as 
a tuple (remote URLs also work):

```py
pygv.browse(
  (
    "https://example.com/example.bam",     # data file
    "https://example.com/example.bam.bai"  # index file
  )
)
```

You can use `track` to adjust track properties beyond the defaults:

```py
pygv.browse(
  pygv.track("10x_cov.bw", name="10x coverage", autoscale=True),
)
```

Multiple tracks are supported by adding to the `browse` call:

```py
pygv.browse(
  # track 1
  (
      "https://example.com/example.bam",
      "https://example.com/example.bam.bai"
  ),
  # track 2
  pygv.track("10x_cov.bw", name="10x coverage", autoscale=True),
  # track 3
  pygv.track("genes.bed", name="Genes", color="blue"),
)
```

## development

development requires [uv](https://astral.sh/uv)

```sh
uv run jupyter lab # open notebook with editable install
```

```sh
uv run pytest      # testing
uv run ruff check  # linting
uv run ruff format # formatting
```

modules.name: pygv
documentation: A minimal, scriptable genome browser for python.

functions.name: pygv._api.locus
documentation: Set the initial locus for the browsers in this session.

functions.name: pygv._api.ref
documentation: Set the reference genome for the browsers in this session.

functions.name: pygv._api.browse
documentation:
Create a new genome browser instance.

Parameters
----------
tracks : tuple[TrackArgument, ...]
    A list of tracks to display in the browser.

Returns
-------
Browser
    The browser widget.

functions.name: pygv._api.load
documentation: Load an existing IGV configuration from a file-like.

functions.name: pygv._api.loads
documentation: Load a JSON-encoded IGV configuration.

functions.name: pygv._config.resolve_file_or_url
documentation:
Resolve a file path or URL to a URL.

Parameters
----------
path_or_url : str | pathlib.Path
    A file path or URL.

Returns
-------
str
    A URL. If `path_or_url` is a URL, it is returned as-is, otherwise
    a file resource is created and the URL is returned.

classes.name: pygv._config.Config

methods.name: pygv._config.Config.servable
documentation: Returns a new config with tracks that are ensured to be servable.

class_variables.name: pygv._config.Config.genome
documentation: noqa: FA100

class_variables.name: pygv._config.Config.locus
documentation: noqa: FA100

class_variables.name: pygv._config.Config.show_sample_names
documentation: noqa: FA100
An IGV configuration.

classes.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.url
documentation: URL to the track data resource, such as a file or webservice, or a data URI.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.name
documentation: Display name (label). Required.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.index_url
documentation:
URL to a file index, such as a BAM .bai, tabix .tbi, or tribble .idx file.

For indexed file access the index URL is required, if absent the entire file
will be read.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.source_type
documentation: Type of data source.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.format
documentation: No default. If not specified, format is inferred from file name extension.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.indexed
documentation:
Explicitly indicate whether the resource is indexed.

This flag is redundant if `index_url` is provided. It can be used to load small
BAM files without an index by setting to `False`

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.order
documentation:
Integer value specifying relative order of track position on the screen.

To pin a track to the bottom use a very large value.
If no order is specified, tracks appear in order of their addition.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.color
documentation: CSS color value for track features, e.g. "#ff0000" or "rgb(100,0,100)".

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.height
documentation: Initial height of track viewport in pixels. Default 50.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.min_height
documentation: Minimum height of track in pixels. Default 50.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.max_height
documentation: Maximum height of track in pixels. Default 500.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.visibility_window
documentation:
Maximum window size in base pairs for which indexed annotations or
variants are displayed.

1 MB for variants, 30 KB for alignments, whole chromosome for
other track types.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.removable
documentation: If true a "remove" item is included in the track menu. Default `True`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.headers
documentation:
HTTP headers to include with each request.

For example `{"Authorization": "Bearer cn389ncoiwuencr"}`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.oauth_token
documentation:
OAuth token, or function returning an OAuth token.

The value will be included as a Bearer token with each request.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.id
documentation: An identifier for this track.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.BaseTrack.samples
documentation: Sample names.

Represents a browser track.

For a full configuration options, see the [IGV.js docs](https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Tracks)

classes.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack.display_mode
documentation: Annotation track display mode. Default `"COLLAPSED"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack.expanded_row_height
documentation: Height of each row of features in `"EXPANDED"` mode. Default `30`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack.squished_row_height
documentation: Height of each row of features in `"SQUISHED"` mode. Default `15`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack.name_field
documentation: For GFF/GTF file formats. Name of column 9 to be used for feature label.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack.max_rows
documentation: Maximum number of rows of features to display. Default `500`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack.searchable
documentation:
Whether feature names for this track can be searched. Default `False`.

Does not work for indexed tracks. Use with caution; it is memory intensive.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack.searchable_fields
documentation:
Field (column 9) names to be included in feature searches.

For use with the `searchable` option in conjunction with GFF files.

When searching for feature attributes spaces need to be escaped with a "+"
sign or percent encoded ("%20).

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack.filter_types
documentation: GFF feature types to filter from display. Default `["chromosome", "gene"]`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack.color
documentation: CSS color value for features. Default `"rgb(0,0,150)"` (i.e. `"#000096"`).

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack.alt_color
documentation: If supplied, used for features on negative strand.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack.color_by
documentation: Used with GFF/GTF files. Name of column 9 attribute to color features by.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AnnotationTrack.color_table
documentation:
Maps attribute values to CSS colors.

Used in conjunction with the `color_by` to assign specific colors to attributes.

Display views of genomic annotations.

Associated file formats: bed, gff, gff3, gtf, bedpe (and more).

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Annotation-Track

Example:
```py
AnnotationTrack(
    name="Color by attribute biotype",
    format="gff3",
    display_mode="EXPANDED",
    height=300,
    url="https://s3.amazonaws.com/igv.org.genomes/hg38/Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.94.chr.gff3.gz",
    index_url="https://s3.amazonaws.com/igv.org.genomes/hg38/Homo_sapiens.GRCh38.94.chr.gff3.gz.tbi",
    visibility_window=1000000,
    color_by="biotype",
    color_table={
        "antisense": "blueviolet",
        "protein_coding": "blue",
        "retained_intron": "rgb(0, 150, 150)",
        "processed_transcript": "purple",
        "processed_pseudogene": "#7fff00",
        "unprocessed_pseudogene": "#d2691e",
        "*": "black"
    }
)
```

classes.name: pygv._tracks.GuideLine

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.GuideLine.color
documentation: A CSS color value.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.GuideLine.dotted
documentation: Whether the line should be dashed.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.GuideLine.y
documentation: The y position. Should be between min and max.
Represents a horizontal guide line.

classes.name: pygv._tracks.WigTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.WigTrack.autoscale
documentation: Autoscale track to maximum value in view.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.WigTrack.autoscale_group
documentation:
An identifier for an autoscale group.

Tracks with the same identifier are autoscaled together.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.WigTrack.min
documentation: Minimum value for the data (y-axis) scale. Usually zero.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.WigTrack.max
documentation: Maximum value for the data (y-axis) scale. Ignored if `autoscale` is `True`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.WigTrack.color
documentation: CSS color value. Default `"rgb(150,150,150)"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.WigTrack.alt_color
documentation: If supplied, used for negative values.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.WigTrack.color_scale
documentation:
Color scale for heatmap (graphType = "heatmap" ).

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Wig-Track/#color-scale-objects

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.WigTrack.guide_lines
documentation: Draw a horizontal line for each object in the given array.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.WigTrack.graph_type
documentation: Type of graph. Default `"bar"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.WigTrack.flip_axis
documentation: Whether the track is drawn "upside down" with zero at top. Default `False`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.WigTrack.window_function
documentation:
Governs how data is summarized when zooming out. Default `"mean"`.

Applicable to tracks created from bigwig and tdf files.

Displays quantititive data as either a bar chart, line plot, or points.

Associated file formats: wig, bigWig, bedGraph.

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Wig-Track/

Example:
```py
WigTrack(
  name="CTCF",
  url="https://www.encodeproject.org/files/ENCFF356YES/@@download/ENCFF356YES.bigWig",
  min="0",
  max="30",
  color="rgb(0, 0, 150)",
  guide_lines=[
    GuideLine(color="green", dotted=True, y=25),
    GuideLine(color="red", dotted=False, y=5),
  ]
)
```

classes.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentSorting

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentSorting.chr
documentation: Sequence (chromosome) name.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentSorting.pos
documentation: Genomic position.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentSorting.option
documentation: Parameter to sort by.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentSorting.tag
documentation: Tag name to sort by. Include only if option = 'TAG

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentSorting.direction
documentation: Sort directions.
Represents initial sort order of packed alignment rows.

classes.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentFiltering

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentFiltering.vendor_failed
documentation: Filter alignments marked as failing vendor quality checks (bit 0x200).

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentFiltering.duplicates
documentation: Filter alignments marked as a duplicate (bit 0x400).

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentFiltering.secondary
documentation: Filter alignments marked as secondary (bit 0x100).

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentFiltering.supplementary
documentation: Filter alignments marked as supplementary (bit 0x800).

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentFiltering.mq
documentation: Filter alignments with mapping quality less than the supplied value.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentFiltering.readgroups
documentation: Read groups ('RG' tag). If present, filter alignments not matching this set.
Represents filtering options for alignments.

classes.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.show_coverage
documentation: Show coverage depth track. Default `True`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.show_alignments
documentation: Show individual alignments. Default `True`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.view_as_pairs
documentation: Whether paired reads are drawn connected with a line. Default `False`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.pairs_supported
documentation: Whether paired mate info is ignored during downsampling. Default `True`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.color
documentation: Default color of alignment blocks. Default `"rgb(170, 170, 170)"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.deletion_color
documentation: Color of line representing a deletion. Default `"black"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.skipped_color
documentation:
Color of line representing a skipped region (e.g., splice junction).

Default `"rgb(150, 170, 170)"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.insertion_color
documentation: Color of marker for insertions. Default `"rgb(138, 94, 161)"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.neg_strand_color
documentation:
Color of alignment on negative strand. Default `"rgba(150, 150, 230, 0.75)"`.

Applicable if `color_by` = `"strand"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.pos_strand_color
documentation:
Color of alignment or position strand. Default `"rgba(230, 150, 150, 0.75)"`.

Applicable if `color_by` = `"strand"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.pair_connector_color
documentation:
Color of connector line between read pairs ("view as pairs" mode).

Defaults to the alignment color.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.color_by
documentation:
Color alignment by property. Default `"unexpectedPair"`.

See: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Alignment-Track/#colorby-options

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.group_by
documentation:
Group alignments by property.

See: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Alignment-Track/#groupby-options

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.sampling_window_size
documentation: Window (bucket) size for alignment downsampling in base pairs. Default `100`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.sampling_depth
documentation:
Number of alignments to keep per bucket. Default 100.

WARNING: Setting to a high value can freeze the browser when
viewing areas of deep coverage.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.readgroup
documentation: Readgroup ID value (tag 'RG').

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.sort
documentation:
Initial sort option.

See: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Alignment-Track/#sort-option

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.filter
documentation:
Alignment filter options.

See: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Alignment-Track/#filter-options

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.show_soft_clips
documentation: Show soft-clipped regions. Default `False`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.show_mismatches
documentation: Highlight alignment bases which do not match the reference. Default `True`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.show_all_bases
documentation: Show all bases of the read sequence. Default `False`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.show_insertion_text
documentation: Show number of bases for insertions inline when zoomed in. Default `False`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.insertion_text_color
documentation: Color for insertion count text. Default `"white"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.show_deletion_text
documentation: Show number of bases deleted inline when zoomed in. Default `False`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.deletion_text_color
documentation: Color for deletion count text. Default `"black"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.display_mode
documentation:
Display mode for the track. Deault `"EXPANDED"`.

* `EXPANDED` - Pack alignments densely and draw at `alignment_row_height`
* `SQUISHED` - Pack alignments densely and draw at `squished_row_height`
* `FULL`     - Draw 1 alignment per row at `alignment_row_height`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.alignment_row_height
documentation:
Pixel height for each alignment row in `"EXPANDED"` or `"FULL"` display mode.

Default `14`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.squished_row_height
documentation: Pixel height for each alignment row in `"SQUISHED"` display mode. Default `3`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.coverage_color
documentation: Color of coverage track. Default `"rgb(150, 150, 150)"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.coverage_track_height
documentation: Height in pixels of the coverage track. Default `3`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.autoscale
documentation: Autoscale coverage track to maximum value in view. `True` unless `max` is set.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.autoscale_group
documentation:
An identifier for an autoscale group for the coverage track.

Tracks with the same identifier are autoscaled together.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.min
documentation: Minimum value for the data (y-axis) scale. Usually zero.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.AlignmentTrack.max
documentation: Maximum value for the data (y-axis) scale. Ignored if `autoscale` is `True`.

Display views of read alignments from BAM or CRAM files.

Associated file formats: bam, cram.

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Alignment-Track

Example:
```py
AlignmentTrack(
  format="bam",
  name="NA12878",
  url="gs://genomics-public-data/platinum-genomes/bam/NA12878_S1.bam",
  index_url="gs://genomics-public-data/platinum-genomes/bam/NA12878_S1.bam.bai",
)
```

classes.name: pygv._tracks.VariantTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.VariantTrack.display_mode
documentation:
Display option.  Default `"EXPANDED"`.

* 'COLLAPSED' => show variants only
* 'SQUISHED' and 'EXPANDED' => show calls.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.VariantTrack.squished_call_height
documentation: Height of genotype call rows in `"SQUISHED"` mode. Default `1`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.VariantTrack.expanded_call_height
documentation: Height of genotype call rows in EXPANDED mode. Default `10`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.VariantTrack.color
documentation: A CSS color value for a variant.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.VariantTrack.color_by
documentation:
Specify an `INFO` field to color variants by.

Optional, if specified takes precedence over `color` property.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.VariantTrack.color_table
documentation:
Color table mapping `INFO` field values to colors.

Use in conjunction with `color_by`.

Optional, if not specified a color table will be generated.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.VariantTrack.no_call_color
documentation: Color for no-calls. Default `"rgb(250, 250, 250)"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.VariantTrack.homevar_color
documentation: CSS color for homozygous non-reference calls. Default `"rgb(17,248,254)"`

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.VariantTrack.hetvar_color
documentation: CSS color for heterozygous calls. Default `"rgb(34,12,253)"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.VariantTrack.homref_color
documentation: CSS color for homozygous reference calls. Default `"rgb(200, 200, 200)"`.

Displays variant records from "VCF" files or equivalents.

Associated file formats: vcf.

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Variant-Track/

Example:
```py
# Basic

VariantTrack(
    format="vcf",
    url="https://s3.amazonaws.com/1000genomes/release/20130502/ALL.chr22.phase3_shapeit2_mvncall_integrated_v5a.20130502.genotypes.vcf.gz",
    index_url="https://s3.amazonaws.com/1000genomes/release/20130502/ALL.chr22.phase3_shapeit2_mvncall_integrated_v5a.20130502.genotypes.vcf.gz.tbi",
    name="1KG variants (chr22)",
    squished_call_height=1,
    expanded_call_height=4,
    display_mode="SQUISHED",
    visibility_window=1000
)

# Color-by info field with color table

VariantTrack(
    url="https://s3.amazonaws.com/igv.org.demo/nstd186.GRCh38.variant_call.vcf.gz",
    index_url="https://s3.amazonaws.com/igv.org.demo/nstd186.GRCh38.variant_call.vcf.gz.tbi",
    name="Color by table, SVTYPE",
    visibility_window=-1,
    color_by="SVTYPE",
    color_table={
        "DEL": "#ff2101",
        "INS": "#001888",
        "DUP": "#028401",
        "INV": "#008688",
        "CNV": "#8931ff",
        "BND": "#891100",
        "*": "#002eff",
    },
)
```

classes.name: pygv._tracks.MutationTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.MutationTrack.display_mode
documentation: The track display mode. Default `"EXPANDED"`.

Displays data from the National Cancer Institute's "mut" and "maf" file formats.

Associated file formats: mut, maf.

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Mutation-Track

Example:
```py
MutationTrack(
    format="maf",
    url="https://s3.amazonaws.com/igv.org.demo/TCGA.BRCA.mutect.995c0111-d90b-4140-bee7-3845436c3b42.DR-10.0.somatic.maf.gz",
    indexed=False,
    height=700,
    display_mode="EXPANDED",
)
```

classes.name: pygv._tracks.SegmentedCopyNumberSorting

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SegmentedCopyNumberSorting.chr
documentation: Sequence (chromosome) name.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SegmentedCopyNumberSorting.start
documentation: Position start.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SegmentedCopyNumberSorting.end
documentation: Position end.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SegmentedCopyNumberSorting.direction
documentation: Sort direction.
Represents initial sort order of segmented copy number rows.

classes.name: pygv._tracks.SegmentedCopyNumberTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SegmentedCopyNumberTrack.display_mode
documentation:
Track display mode.

Affects the sample height (height of each row). The "FILL" value will result in all
samples visible in the track view.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SegmentedCopyNumberTrack.sort
documentation: The initial sort order.

Displays segmented copy number values as a heatmap.

Associated file formats: seg.

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Seg-Track

* Red = amplifications
* Blue = deletions

There are 2 common conventions for values in segmented copy number files,
the copy number itself, and a log score computed from:

```py
score = 2 * np.log2(copy_number / 2)
```

The value type is indicated by the `is_log` property.

If no value is set for `is_log`, it is inferred by the values in the file:

* all positive values => `is_log` = `False`
* any negative values => `is_log` = `True`

Example:
```py
SegmentedCopyNumberTrack(
    format="seg",
    url="https://s3.amazonaws.com/igv.org.demo/GBM-TP.seg.gz",
    indexed=False,
    is_log=True,
    name="GBM Copy # (TCGA Broad GDAC)",
    sort=SegmentedCopyNumberSorting(
        direction="DESC",
        chr="chr7",
        start=55174641,
        end=55175252,
    ),
)
```

classes.name: pygv._tracks.GwasColumns

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.GwasColumns.chromosome
documentation: Chromosome number

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.GwasColumns.position
documentation: Genomic position

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.GwasColumns.value
documentation: Value.
Declaration of column number for chrom, position, & value.

classes.name: pygv._tracks.GwasTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.GwasTrack.min
documentation: Sets the minimum value for the data (y-axis) scale. Default `0`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.GwasTrack.max
documentation:
Sets the maximum value for the data (y-axis) scale.

Default `25` for p-value (-log10(pvalue)), `1` for posterior probability.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.GwasTrack.posterior_probability
documentation:
Whether to interpret values as probabilities within range 0-1. Default `False`.

By default values are treated as p-values and plotted as -log10(P-value).

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.GwasTrack.dot_size
documentation: Diameter of dots in pixels. Default `3`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.GwasTrack.columns
documentation:
Declaration of column number for chromosome, position, and value.

For gwas format only.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.GwasTrack.color_table
documentation:
Object mapping chromosome names -> colors.

If supplied all chromosomes in data should be included.

See defaults: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/GWAS/#default-color-table

Display genome wide association data as a "manhattan" style plot.

Associated file formats: bed, gwas.

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/GWAS

Example:
```py
GwasTrack(
    format="gwas",
    name="GWAS sample",
    url="https://s3.amazonaws.com/igv.org.demo/gwas_sample.tsv.gz",
    indexed=False,
    columns=GwasColumns(
        chromosome=12,
        position=13,
        value=28,
    ),
)
```

classes.name: pygv._tracks.InteractTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.InteractTrack.arc_type
documentation:
The arc type. Default `"nested"`.

* nested - Arc height is proportional to feature width.
* propotional - Arc height is proportional to feature score.
* inView - Proportional, only draw arcs that are completely in view.
* partialInView - Proportional, only draw arcs that are whole or partially in view.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.InteractTrack.arc_orientation
documentation: Direction of arcs ("UP" or "DOWN"). Default `"UP"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.InteractTrack.alpha
documentation:
Alpha transparency to apply to arcs that extend beyond viewport.

Must be between `0` and `1`. Default `0.5`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.InteractTrack.thickness
documentation: Line thickness. Default `2`.

Display pairwise interactions between genome regions as arcs.

Associated file formats: bedpe, interact, bigInteract

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Interact

Example:
```py
Config(
    genome="hg38",
    locus="chr2:65,489,209-65,795,733",
    tracks=[
        InteractTrack(
            url="https://s3.amazonaws.com/igv.org.demo/GSM1872886_GM12878_CTCF_PET.bedpe.txt",
            format="bedpe",
            name="CTCF PET - proportional",
            arc_type="proportional",
            arc_orientation="UP",
            color="rgb(0,200,0)",
            log_scale=true,
            max=80,
            visibility_window=10_000_000,
        ),
        InteractTrack(
            url="https://s3.amazonaws.com/igv.org.demo/GSM1872886_GM12878_CTCF_PET.bedpe.txt",
            format="bedpe",
            name="CTCF PET - nested",
            arc_type="nested",
            arc_orientation="DOWN",
            color="blue",
            alpha=0.15,
            visibility_window=10_000_000,
        ),
    ],
)
```

classes.name: pygv._tracks.QtlTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.QtlTrack.min
documentation: Minimum value of y-axis in -log10 units. Default `3.5`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.QtlTrack.max
documentation:
Maximum value of y-axis in -log10 units.

Optional, if not specified max is set as a percentile of values in view.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.QtlTrack.autoscale_percentile
documentation:
Upper percentile for setting max value when autoscaling.

Number between `0` and `100`. Default `98`.

Displays xQTL data.

Associated file formats: qtl

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/QTL-Track

Example:
```py
QtlTrack(
    format="qtl",
    name="B cell eQTL",
    url="https://igv-genepattern-org.s3.amazonaws.com/test/qtl/B.cell_eQTL.tsv.gz",
    index_url="https://igv-genepattern-org.s3.amazonaws.com/test/qtl/B.cell_eQTL.tsv.gz.tbi",
    visibility_window=4_000_000,
)
```

classes.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.color_by
documentation: Splice junction color. Default `"numUniqueReads"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.color_by_num_reads_threshold
documentation:
Threshold for `color_by`. Default `5`.

If `color_by` is set to `"numUniqueReads"` or `"numReads"`, junction color will
be darker when number of reads exceeds this threshold.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.thickness_based_on
documentation: Splice junction line thickness. Default `"numUniqueReads"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.bounce_height_based_on
documentation: Splice junction curve height. Default `"random"`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.label_unique_read_count
documentation: Add unique read counts to splice junction label. Default `True`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.label_multi_mapped_read_count
documentation: Add multi-mapped read counts to splice junction label. Default `True`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.label_total_read_count
documentation: Add total read counts to splice junction label. Default `False`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.label_motif
documentation: Add splice junction motif to its label. Default `False`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.label_annotated_junction
documentation:
Label annotation for junction.

If defined, the string will be appended to the labels of splice junctions that exist
in known gene models.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.min_uniquely_mapped_reads
documentation:
Junction must be supported by at least this many uniquely-mapped reads.

Default `0`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.min_total_reads
documentation:
Junction must be supported by at least this many uniquely-mapped + multi-mapped reads.

Default `0`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.max_fraction_multi_mapped_reads
documentation:
(Uniquely-mapped reads) / (Total reads) must be <= this threshold.

Default `1`.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.min_spliced_alignment_overhang
documentation:
Minimum spliced alignment overhang in base pairs. Default `0`.

See [STAR aligner docs](https://github.com/alexdobin/STAR/blob/master/doc/STARmanual.pdf) for details.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.hide_strand
documentation: Set to "+" or "-" to hide junctions on the plus or minus strand.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.hide_annotated_junctions
documentation:
Whether to hide annotated junctions. Default `False`.

If `True`, only novel junctions will be shown (e.g., those not found in gene models passed to the aligner).

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.hide_unannotated_junctions
documentation:
Whether to hide unannotated junctions. Default `False`.

If `True`, only annotated junctions will be shown (eg. those found in gene models passed to the aligner).

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.SpliceJunctionTrack.hide_motifs
documentation:
A list of strings for motif values to hide.

For example: ["GT/AT", "non-canonical"]

Displays splice junction information.

Associated file formats: bed.

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Splice-Junctions

Example:
```python
SpliceJunctionTrack(
    name="Junctions",
    format="bed",
    url="https://www.dropbox.com/s/nvmy55hhe24plpv/splice_junction_track_test_cases_sampleA.chr15-92835700-93031800.SJ.out.bed.gz?dl=0",
    index_url="https://www.dropbox.com/s/iv5tcg3t8v3xu23/splice_junction_track_test_cases_sampleA.chr15-92835700-93031800.SJ.out.bed.gz.tbi?dl=0",
    display_mode="COLLAPSED",
    min_uniquely_mapped_reads=1,
    min_total_reads=1,
    max_fraction_multi_mapped_reads=1,
    min_spliced_alignment_overhang=0,
    thickness_based_on="numUniqueReads",
    bounce_height_based_on="random",
    color_by="isAnnotatedJunction",
    label_unique_read_count=True,
    label_multi_mapped_read_count=True,
    label_total_read_count=False,
    label_motif=False,
    label_is_annotated_junction=" [A]",
    hide_annotated_junctions=False,
    hide_unannotated_junctions=False,
    hide_motifs=["GT/AT", "non-canonical"],
)
```

classes.name: pygv._tracks.CnvPytorTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.CnvPytorTrack.signal_name
documentation:
Signal name. Default `"rd_nsp"`

* rd_snp : Read Depth and BAF Likelihood
* rd : Read depth
* snp : BAF likelihood

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.CnvPytorTrack.cnv_caller
documentation:
Name of CNV caller. Default `"2D"`.

Shows data based on available caller data.

* ReadDepth: Uses Read depth information only
* 2D: Uses both Read depth and BAF information

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.CnvPytorTrack.bin_size
documentation:
Bin size. Default `100_000`.

* pytor file: Bin size should be avialable in the pytor file
* vcf: Bin size should be multiple of 10,000

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.CnvPytorTrack.colors
documentation:
Color of the signals. Signal details are in file format section.

Default `["gray", "black", "green", "blue"]`.

Displays read depth and B-allele frequency (BAF) of variants.

Associated file formats: pytor, vcf

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/CNVPytor

Example:
```python
CnvPytorTrack(
    id="pytor_track",
    name="HepG2 pytor",
    url="https://storage.googleapis.com/cnvpytor_data/HepG2_WGS.pytor",
)
```

classes.name: pygv._tracks.MergedTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.MergedTrack.tracks
documentation: Child wig tracks.

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.MergedTrack.alpha
documentation:
Alpha transparency to apply to individual track colors.

Number between `0` and `1`. Default `0.5`.

Overlay multiple wig tracks.

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Merged/

Example:
```py
MergedTrack(
    name="Merged",
    height=50,
    alpha=0.5,
    tracks=[
        WigTrack(
            format="bigwig",
            url="https://www.encodeproject.org/files/ENCFF000ASJ/@@download/ENCFF000ASJ.bigWig",
            color="red",
        ),
        WigTrack(
            format="bigwig",
            url="https://www.encodeproject.org/files/ENCFF351WPV/@@download/ENCFF351WPV.bigWig",
            color="green",
        ),
    ],
)
```

classes.name: pygv._tracks.ArcTrack

class_variables.name: pygv._tracks.ArcTrack.arc_orientation
documentation: Direction of arcs ("UP" or "DOWN"). Default `"UP"`.

Displays RNA secondary structures in arcs connecting base pairs.

Associated file formats: bp, bed

Ref: https://igv.org/doc/igvjs/#tracks/Arc-Track

Alternative structures, where one nucleotide is involved in more than one base pair,
and pseudo knots, where arcs cross, can be accommodated.

Example:
```py
ArcTrack(format="bp", name="RNA Struct BP", url="example.bp")
```
